original_hypothesis.title: AgentFactoryV4 can create a Uniswap pair but not record it in AgentNftV2 virtualLPs for custom token path if downstream assumptions expect AgentToken-style liquidityPools()
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In AgentFactoryV4 custom token execution, lp is set from _createPair(token) and liquidity is added via router.addLiquidity, but unlike the AgentToken path there is no canonical AgentToken contract that tracks liquidityPools(). Any downstream components that assume the LP address can be discovered via IAgentToken(token).liquidityPools()[0] may fail or become inconsistent. This can enable griefing (breaking staking initialization or UI assumptions) if custom token applications are used widely.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a practical integration defect: the system supports two paths (new AgentToken vs custom token). The custom token path bypasses AgentToken bookkeeping, increasing the chance of mismatched assumptions across staking, DAO initialization, or indexing services.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Custom token path sets lp = _createPair(token) and adds liquidity, but does not create an AgentToken instance to expose liquidityPools()

preconditions: Custom token application path is used
Downstream components or off-chain indexers assume AgentToken semantics for discovering LP

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: divergent execution paths may violate implicit interface assumptions
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: LP discoverability and consistent wiring across components
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No explicit event for custom LP creation; no registry mapping token->lp beyond AgentNft mint payload

analysis_questions: Do any contracts (veToken, DAO, rewards) rely on IAgentToken(token).liquidityPools() for LP discovery?
Is AgentNftV2.virtualLPs[virtualId].pool always used as the single source of truth, and is it correctly set for both paths?
Are there differences in LP staking token selection between paths that can cause stake() to fail?
validation_requirements: Trace LP address usage across the system (especially in veToken initialization and staking asset)
Simulate custom token execution end-to-end and check all components can operate
Confirm events/state enable off-chain consumers to correctly index LP/token relationships

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A malicious proposer uses the custom token path to create an agent whose LP is not discoverable by components expecting AgentToken.liquidityPools(), causing staking/governance tooling failures and effectively DoSing participation.
prerequisites: Ability to submit and execute a custom token application

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Moderate: disruption of staking/governance participation and potential loss of user confidence; indirect economic harm
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: High via tooling fixes if core on-chain state is correct

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No visible integration tests for the custom token application path

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Compare on-chain virtualLPs.pool vs any consumer lookups

validation_result.mechanism: speculative integration concern, not actual code defect
validation_result.impact_summary: The LP address is correctly recorded in AgentNftV2.virtualLPs for both the AgentToken and custom token paths. No on-chain component in scope relies on IAgentToken(token).liquidityPools() for LP discovery after creation.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis claims that the custom token path in AgentFactoryV4 fails to record the LP address in a way that downstream components can discover it. However, tracing the actual code paths shows this is incorrect.

In `_executeApplication` (AgentFactoryV4.sol), both paths produce an `lp` variable that is passed to `IAgentNft(nft).mint(virtualId, _vault, ..., lp, token)`. Inside `AgentNftV2.mint()`, the LP is stored in `virtualLPs[virtualId].pool`. This is the canonical source of truth.

The veToken is initialized with `lp` as the `stakingAsset` parameter in both paths, so staking works correctly regardless of the token type.

The only place `IAgentToken(token).liquidityPools()` is called is within `_executeApplication` itself (line ~229) to get the LP address for the NEW AgentToken path. For the custom token path, the LP comes directly from `_createPair(token)`. No other on-chain code in scope calls `liquidityPools()` on the agent's token after creation.

The hypothesis is based on speculative assumptions about downstream components that might use `liquidityPools()`, but no such components exist in the provided codebase.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code correctly handles LP recording for both paths. In AgentFactoryV4.sol `_executeApplication`, both the AgentToken path and custom token path produce an `lp` address that flows into `IAgentNft(nft).mint(...)`, which stores it in `virtualLPs[virtualId].pool` (AgentNftV2.sol line in mint function). The veToken is initialized with this LP in both paths via `_createNewAgentVeToken(..., lp, ...)`. No on-chain consumer in scope uses `IAgentToken(token).liquidityPools()` for LP discovery post-creation.

calling_sequence: Custom token path: lp = _createPair(token) - LP created via Uniswap factory
addLiquidity called on router with token, assetToken
veToken = _createNewAgentVeToken(..., lp, ...) - veToken initialized with LP as stakingAsset
IAgentNft(nft).mint(virtualId, _vault, ..., dao, proposer, cores, lp, token) - LP stored in virtualLPs[virtualId].pool
IAgentVeToken(veToken).stake(...) - LP staked successfully using the correct LP address
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: There is no vulnerable code path. Both the new AgentToken path and the custom token path correctly produce and store the LP address in AgentNftV2.virtualLPs[virtualId].pool. The LP is also correctly used as the staking asset for the veToken. The hypothesis's concern about IAgentToken(token).liquidityPools() not being available on custom tokens is irrelevant because no on-chain consumer in scope uses that function for LP discovery.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided for AgentFactoryV4 specifically, but the hypothesis is not config-dependent.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario describes a proposer using the custom token path to create an agent whose LP is not discoverable. However, the LP IS discoverable via `AgentNftV2.virtualLP(virtualId).pool`, which is the canonical on-chain registry. The staking system uses the LP address passed during veToken initialization (not discovered from the token contract), so staking works correctly. The DAO is initialized with the veToken, which correctly backs LP tokens. No on-chain component fails due to the custom token not having a `liquidityPools()` method.
validation_result.impact_assessment: No actual impact. The system correctly handles both token paths. The LP address is stored in AgentNftV2.virtualLPs for both paths. The veToken staking system works correctly because it uses the LP address provided during initialization, not discovered from the token. Off-chain indexing concerns are speculative and outside the scope of a code defect analysis.
recommended_mitigations: Consider adding custom tokens to allTradingTokens for indexing consistency (minor improvement, not a security fix)
Consider emitting a dedicated event for custom token LP creation for better off-chain indexing
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy AgentFactoryV4 with all dependencies. 2. Call initFromToken with a compatible ERC20 token. 3. Call executeTokenApplication. 4. Verify AgentNftV2.virtualLP(virtualId).pool returns the correct LP address. 5. Verify veToken.assetToken() returns the correct LP address. 6. Verify staking and governance work correctly with the custom token agent.
uncertainties: Off-chain indexers or UI components not in scope may assume IAgentToken semantics, but this is outside the smart contract code review scope
Future on-chain components added to the system might make assumptions about liquidityPools() availability
additional_testing_needed: End-to-end integration test of the custom token application path to confirm all components interoperate correctly
Verify that custom token agents can participate in governance and staking without issues
